description: Raised in the Alterac Mountains by the Stormpike Clan, trained to headbutt orcs.

name: Blue Marsh Hopper
description: That squishing sound every time it takes a step? That's a feature.

name: Yellow Marsh Hopper
description: No one makes giant, slimy spawns like Krag'wa the Huge.

name: Deepcoral Snapdragon
description: The snapdragon is a quiet hunter in the water, but is far less stealthy on land.

name: Snapdragon Kelpstalker
description: When a snapdragon bites you, the worry isn't about the bite itself, but the poison that will begin to eat away at the wound.

name: Wonderwing 2.0
description: Don't ask what happened to the first one -- Mechagon technology is still gnome technology!

name: Crimson Tidestallion
description: The blood-red coloration of these creatures is ideal for hiding in large coral formations.

name: Child of Torcali
description: This daughter of the great direhorn loa, Torcali, never lost faith that her keeper would help her grow to her full potential.

name: Stonehide Elderhorn
description: Stonehide elderhorns, one of the few types of moose to inhabit island climates, have thinner fur than most elderhorns.

name: Bloodthirsty Dreadwing
description: The forsaken prefer the use of these bloodthirsty hunters, both for their speed as a mount, and the psychological effect they have on their enemies.

name: Inkscale Deepseeker
description: Schools of tidestallions will viciously slam their muscular chests into their prey to take them down.

name: Bloodgorged Hunter
description: If he wasn't so full, you wouldn't have caught him.

name: Risen Mare
description: Something terrible happened in the lost town of Havenswood.

name: Junkheap Drifter
description: When you really want to be noticed... and don't mind strapping yourself to a giant, barely-held-together wheel.

name: Silent Glider
description: Gasses naturally stored within the creature allowed it to survive within the depths, those same gasses also allow them to effortlessly float through the air.

name: Azureshell Krolusk
description: Captured from the desolate sands of Vol'dun, this mount is surprisingly docile in captivity.

name: Royal Snapdragon
description: Captured at a young age and raised to serve naga hunters, snapdragons are loyal and fierce companions.

name: Snapback Scuttler
description: Giant crabs are native to Nazjatar and a favored food of the naga. This one must have been well-kept to grow this large.

name: Mechagon Mechanostrider
description: The best mount, logically, serves multiple purposes -- sometimes you need to get places AND reach something high up once you get there.

name: Rubyshell Krolusk
description: Captured from the desolate sands of Vol'dun, this mount is surprisingly docile in captivity.

name: Priestess' Moonsaber
description: Once these elegant cats were only given to high-ranking priestesses of Elune, but now they ride into battle with all manner of champion, in the service of vengeance.

name: Swift Spectral Fathom Ray
description: Even the dead need a lift every now and then.

name: Swift Spectral Magnetocraft
description: Even the dead need a lift every now and then.

name: Swift Spectral Armored Gryphon
description: Even the dead need a lift every now and then.

name: Swift Spectral Pterrordax
description: Even the dead need a lift every now and then.

name: Blackpaw
description: Blackpaw was just hibernating, minding his own business, and then suddenly there was a warfront.

name: Ashenvale Chimaera
description: Ashenvale chimaeras were used frequently by the Kaldorei to decimate structures in battles against the humans, orcs, and undead.

name: Frightened Kodo
description: Nothing frightens a kodo like seeing a bear with antlers.

name: Umber Nightsaber
description: Nightsabers are an integral part of the Kaldorei's army, serving as both mount and assault weapon in one.

name: Kul Tiran Charger
description: The Kul Tiran military prides itself on mastery of both sea, through a vast navy, and land, with armed cavalry.

name: Sandy Nightsaber
description: A rare color for a nightsaber, and rarely seen, they are prized amongst huntresses.

name: Kaldorei Nightsaber
description: The connection between a nightsaber and its hunter or huntress is nearly inseparable.

name: Explorer's Jungle Hopper
description: For when you're making a crash landing in the middle of an uncharted swamp because it's the only way to access that ancient temple.

name: Explorer's Dunetrekker
description: The surly dunetrekker can easily carry a set of explorers to the nearest ruins and back without the need to rest.

name: Gargantuan Grrloc
description: It's the biggest murloc you've ever seen!

name: Bruce
description: BRUCE!

name: X-995 Mechanocat
description: The X-995 is the ultimate in customizeable feline robo-companionship.

name: Uncorrupted Voidwing
description: This spawn of Vexiona, cleansed by Wrathion, comes from a flight with a fractured past and uncertain future.

name: Caravan Hyena
description: The canopy atop this dune-wanderer can be stored at your campsite, should you desire to feel the wind in your fur.

name: Ankoan Waveray
description: The Ankoan have been battling the naga for years, and with a natural affinity for the sea, training the local wildlife was a natural strategy.

name: Azshari Bloatray
description: The naga raised these rays in captivity, harvesting their gas organs to craft alchemy which allowed them to breathe air more easily.

name: Bloodflank Charger
description: Stolen from the Arathi Basin stables, this fire-hearted charger now steeds for the Horde.

name: Ironclad Frostclaw
description: Captured in the snows of Alterac Valley, this bloodthirsty hunter now roars for the Alliance.

name: Squeakers, the Trickster
description: The locals in Halfhill swear that whenever a batch of grain is missing, a farmworking instrument is misplaced, or a bushel of berries can't be found, you can hear a mischievous squeaking, and are reminded to slow down and accept what life gives you.

name: Hopecrusher Gargon
description: Once the loyal mount of a House Briarbane lord, it turned on the lord and his retinue. Members of House Briarbane now whisper of a dark Gargon lying in wait even baiting venthyr who venture through the darker parts of the Chalice District of Revendreth.

name: Battle Gargon Vrednic
description: Prince Renathal's loyal gargon, Vrednic has been by his side for millennia. Adorned in his Sinvyr armor, Vrednic's mere presence instills terror to all those who lay eyes on him as he ushers his rider into battle.

name: Obsidian Worldbreaker
description: "I have had many names throughout history, all of them spoken with dread: Neltharion, Xaxas, and many more. Yet you shall know me best as Deathwing, for so I am! I am the bane of life, the darkness within history, the lord of death, the master of destruction. And I tell you now, and so it is true, that this world is mine!" - Neltharion, the Earth-Warder

name: Lucky Yun
description: The Pandaren believe that merely rubbing one's hands on the shiny hide of the ox will grant prosperity, the challenge is catching it.

name: Alabaster Stormtalon
description: Chiseled by the stonemasons of Stormwind, this stone gryphon symbolizes the unbending courage of the Alliance.

name: Alabaster Thunderwing
description: Chiseled by the stoneworkers of Orgrimmar, this stone wind rider is a symbol of the unwavering strength of the Horde.

name: Unshackled Waveray
description: The Unshackled have a natural kinship with the species of the deep seas that have seen cruelty at the hands of the naga.

name: Honeyback Hivemother
description: Destined to become queen of her hive, she scouts Kul Tiras and Zandalar in search of a suitable home for a new colony to flourish.

name: Glacial Tidestorm
description: Though she commands a variety of magical powers, Jaina has always been most proficient at summoning powerful elementals of water and ice.

name: Dreamlight Runestag
description: Runestags are granted to those who have pledged their service to the Winter Queen and the Court of Night.

name: Enchanted Dreamlight Runestag
description: The majestic runestags are capable of siphoning anima from the ambient light of Ardenweald with their elaborate antlers. The anima is used as nourishment and to power the runestag's speed and magic.

name: Mawsworn Soulhunter
description: Soulhunters are used to track wayward souls across the wastes that flee from their dark fates, and are one of the only creatures that feel comfortable traversing the maw.

name: Darkwarren Hardshell
description: The Darkwarren Hardshell's chitin is sturdy enough to withstand mortar shells and dragon's fire. It will also conveniently consume anything lacking anima, if needed.

name: Swift Gloomhoof
description: The ballads of Ardenweald claim that the birth of gloomrunner heralds the passing of a particularly benevolent wild god. Seeing a gloomrunner is therefore a blessing and a curse.

name: Sundancer
description: This sun-dappled courser gallops on lightbeams with carefree abandon.

name: Chestnut Courser
description: Coursers make for excellent steeds. Just don't try to dismount from their front.

name: Chittering Animite
description: These exceptionally agile fliers make excellent mounts once they've been trained not to sap every ounce of anima from their rider.

name: Hogrus, Swine of Good Fortune
description: Hogrus was first seen by Emperor Shaohao on the third day of his meditation, and is considered by the Pandaren to be a sign of good fortune to come to those who take action.

name: Vulpine Familiar
description: A fiercely intelligent and curious species, these fox-like creatures have travelled the Great Dark Beyond and found their way to Azeroth. Drawn to Azeroth's champions, they seek adventure and companionship.

name: Sylverian Dreamer
description: An artist in the magical city of Dalaran fell asleep on her brush, and dreamed of painting a beautiful feathered dragon. Her artwork leapt from the page, and when she awoke, she found it sleeping gently beside her.

name: Horrid Dredwing
description: Harika the Horrid terrorized the denizens of the Forest Ward, and with her demise her broodlings can now take this horror to denizens farther afield.

name: Ensorcelled Everwyrm
description: Anima is a fundamental force that infuses the Shadowlands. It flows through many creatures, but is also traded, bartered for, and fought over.

name: Crusader's Direhorn
description: A loyal steed to the holy crusaders of Zandalar.

name: Mail Muncher
description: Worming its way through the Postmaster's network, this serpent of N'Zoth deprived Azeroth of its primary magical correspondence.

name: Sunwarmed Furline
description: The presence of the giant felines heralds the beginning of the end for the birds of Azeroth.

name: Silky Shimmermoth
description: The moths of Ardenweald play a vital role in the ecosystem, carrying spores, anima, and pollen from one part of the forest to the other, providing nourishment and variation to the denizens living below.

name: Drake of the Four Winds
description: Rocks erode, fires sputter, but wind remains powerful regardless of the passage of time. -Phaoris

name: Rajani Warserpent
description: Fierce companions of the followers of Ra-den, the Rajani Warserpents are a force to be reckoned with.

name: Restoration Deathwalker
description: With the death of the Jailer and the ascension of a new Arbiter, the Shadowlands is returning to its great Purpose. Some deathwalkers recognize the role that mortal heroes have played, and are willing to serve as mounts.

name: Warstitched Darkhound
description: These warhounds are crafted by the House of Constructs to carry any rider into the thick of battle, rending and tearing enemy mounts all the while.

name: Bulbous Necroray
description: The necrorays of Maldraxxus prefer to congregate around the enormous decaying mushrooms of the region.

name: Infested Necroray
description: Necrorays lay their eggs in small clusters within the frills and hollows of decaying fungal blooms.

name: Bound Shadehound
description: Created by deciphering and performing powerful runic magic, this Shadehound is bound to its creator.

name: Corridor Creeper
description: The loyal hounds of the Jailer creep along the corridors in search of the scent of souls.

name: Snowstorm
description: He comes from the ice and snow, under the Azeroth sun where adventurers go.

name: Desertwing Hunter
description: In service of the great Purpose, desertwing hunters were designed to fill distant skies of forgotten realms of the Shadowlands.

name: Crusty Crawler
description: This rare giant crab has taken a break from its nautical nonsense deep under the sea to explore the surface.

name: Wild Glimmerfur Prowler
description: This prowler's brilliant green coat distinguishes it from the blues and purples of Ardenweald, making it much easier for others of its kind to see through the midnight woods.

name: Wildseed Cradle
description: The most precious cargo in all Ardenweald, the wildseeds, are cradled by this lovingly-crafted cart. It is imbued with anima to allow it to hover over the ground.

name: Mawsworn Charger
description: The fate of all horse mounts.

name: Hand of Hrestimorak
description: The treachery of Hrestimorak was infamous, betraying his people to claim power. Facing damnation in the Maw, he tried to subvert the Jailer... a foolish act that would earn him an eternity of torment.

name: Shadeleaf Runestag
description: The shadeleaf runestag are bred for their ferocity as well as their beauty by the Wild Hunt. They feel no fear charging into the den of any predator.

name: Wakener's Runestag
description: Runestags enjoy lying near wildseeds. The Wakeners believe they can hear the sleepers' dreams. These runestags display a range of emotions in their vigil, signaling the dreams or nightmares of the spirits within.

name: Winterborn Runestag
description: Winterborn runestags are blessed by the Winter Queen herself. Their runes flare brilliantly when predators are nearby, warning the entire herd.

name: Enchanted Shadeleaf Runestag
description: A shadeleaf runestag is capable of goring creatures as powerful as a gorm matriarch in defense of its herd. The Wild Hunt ride these mounts into dangerous corners of the Shadowlands, when their domain must be defended from those who would threaten the wilds.

name: Enchanted Wakener's Runestag
description: Wakener runestags fiercely protect their wildseed groves. When the groves starting to fail, many runestags went berserk.

name: Enchanted Winterborn Runestag
description: Winterborn runestags are the favored mount of the Winter Queen herself, and she only bestows such a creature on her most favored subjects.

name: Shimmermist Runner
description: Mischievous spriggans and the valerunners have a natural enmity. No valerunner would deign to allow a spriggan rider because they can sense their inherent malevolence. This angers the trickster spriggans who take twisted enjoyment in playing cruel pranks on these majestic creatures.

name: Duskflutter Ardenmoth
description: These ardenmoths prefer the dark and damp forests of northwest Ardenweald, where fungal colonies are perpetually blooming.

name: Spinemaw Gladechewer
description: Spriggan riders tie bits of anima-less detritus to the end of sticks in front of their gorm mounts to steer. It's... an imperfect art.

name: Umbral Scythehorn
description: Umbral gorm are the most docile of all gorm, but they also grow the largest horns and mandibles. They are probably so docile because other creatures avoid riling them.

name: Pale Regal Cervid
description: Looking at these majestic creatures, it's not difficult to see how the idea of cervids developed into the stags found on Azeroth and other mortal worlds.

name: Silvertip Dredwing
description: Bred by the Revendreth prince, Renathal, these rare dredwing are fiercely loyal and will only let those deemed worthy by the prince himself to interact with them.

name: Phase-Lost Slateback
description: This Slateback got lost wandering into various phases as it followed some ethereals around.

name: Rampart Screecher
description: Roosting among the ramparts of Castle Nathria, these giant dredbats act as sentinels and are trained to take down threats approaching the castle from the air.

name: Harvester's Dredwing
description: The dredwings of the Harvesters fear no mawsworn, and will gleefully sink their teeth into anything that threatens Sinfall.

name: Endmire Flyer
description: Attracted through portals through which little is known these flying devourers have found a home in the Endmire of Revendreth feeding on the latent anima.

name: Inquisition Gargon
description: The gargon of the Inquisitors are some of the most vicious ever carved. The Avowed seek to recapture these beasts and return them to the righteous duty of assisting in the purifying of souls rather than the perversions performed at the hands of the Inquisitors.

name: Sinfall Gargon
description: The gargon are the loyal mounts and companions of the Venthyr and Renathal has found many abandoned at Sinfall, awaiting reawakening and assignment to worthy members of the covenant.

name: Magenta Cloud Serpent
description: More affectionate than their cousins, Magenta Cloud Serpents are known to form close bonds with their riders.

name: Crypt Gargon
description: Loyal hounds of the Curator, these gargon patrol the crypts and catacombs deterring both trespassing and escape.

name: Desire's Battle Gargon
description: The Countess maintains a small stable of elegantly adorned gargon mounts for her sole use. Many a rival has been taken in by the finery to their peril as she personally oversees their deadly training.

name: Gravestone Battle Gargon
description: Representative of a new loyal batch of gargon, these battle ready beasts will take Renathal's most loyal compatriots into the final battles against the Jailer.

name: Battle Gargon Silessa
description: Silessa fought valiantly alongside her master in the great incursion by the Light, following the battle her master set her to guard over a vault room of great value.

name: Lightforged Ruinstrider
description: A loyal steed to the Lightforged Draenei.

name: Loyal Gorger
description: Crawling forth from the cracks between the realms, the devourers have a voracious appetite for anima. Little is known of their origins, but all denizens of the Shadowlands see their recent boldness as a sign of the end times.

name: Phalynx of Loyalty
description: Some within the Ascended keep a phalynx as a pet. While they may not be as furry as a larion, they are much less likely to eat your other pets.

name: Phalynx of Humility
description: The phalynx does not fetch, though it is not known why this is.

name: Phalynx of Courage
description: A phalynx is considered both a worthy companion and a lithe opponent to test one's reflexes against.

name: Phalynx of Purity
description: Little is known about the origination of phalynx technology, the machinations of which are taught more akin to religion than engineering.

name: Eternal Phalynx of Purity
description: The armored phalynx is a favorite companion of the mightiest warriors of the Ascended as it is both fearless and vicious in battle.

name: Eternal Phalynx of Courage
description: Many bars of gildenite are required to forge one of these sparkling mounts, but the sight of them alone is worth the gathering time.

name: Eternal Phalynx of Loyalty
description: A marvel of steward engineering, the armored phalynx is a rare and awe-inspiring sight.

name: Eternal Phalynx of Humility
description: When an armored phalynx is forged, stewards clap their hammers together in reverence of the skill required in its creation.

name: Silverwind Larion
description: This gift of the silvery winds is only bestowed upon those that search high and low for the wisdom of Bastion.

name: Gilded Prowler
description: The mighty larion is an apex predator of Bastion, but that does not stop it from enjoying a nice nap in the sunny fields.

name: Ascended Skymane
description: Only those that have proven themselves against the combined might of the Ascended will be able to ride this mount.

name: Pestilent Necroray
description: When a necroray hatches, spores fly everywhere, making a huge mess.

name: Colossal Slaughterclaw
description: Only the most stalwart stable masters of Maldraxxus are able to break the will of the colossal slaughterclaws, giving all champions of the Undying Army a fearsome ally in the Shadowlands.

name: Vicious War Spider
description: When being a courageous member of the Alliance doesn't strike the right amount of fear into your opponent, add a giant spider.

name: Vicious War Spider
description: When being a bellowing member of the Horde doesn't strike the right amount of fear into your opponent, add a giant spider.

name: War-Bred Tauralus
description: The soldiers of Maldraxxus protect the Shadowlands, and require a mount that is both capable and calm when faced with overwhelming odds.

name: Plaguerot Tauralus
description: The tauralus is considered courageous, though scholars of Maldraxxus have long wondered if their lack of fear is simply due to the tiny size of their brains.

name: Bonehoof Tauralus
description: There are quite a few reasons that you do not want to stand behind a tauralus, the hooves and whip-like tails being the least of them.

name: Chosen Tauralus
description: Even an unarmored tauralus is a valuable ally in battle, its enormous horns capable of piercing even plate armor.

name: Armored War-Bred Tauralus
description: The tauralus that serve the armies of Maldraxxus have had all traits of compassion bred out of them, and are only adorned in armor when they show that no sword or spell can stop them from carrying their rider to victory.

name: Armored Plaguerot Tauralus
description: When fully armored, a tauralus can take an incredible amount of damage before falling, making it a valuable ally in battle.

name: Armored Bonehoof Tauralus
description: A favored mount of the House of the Chosen, the tauralus only gets more angry and vicious when exposed to battle.

name: Armored Chosen Tauralus
description: The tauralus has two tails, which are both absolutely necessary for whipping away buzzing pests like the bloodlouse.

name: Blisterback Bloodtusk
description: The House of the Chosen train their mounts in all manner of difficult circumstances, so that no matter the battle, they will never falter.

name: Gorespine
description: Nerissa trampled over countless bodies on the back of Gorespine as she rose to prominence as the commander of the Lichsworn forces for the House of Rituals.

name: Bonecleaver's Skullboar
description: Sabreil was known for the ruthless efficiency with which she dismembered foes, feeding their bones to her loyal skullboar.

name: Lurid Bloodtusk
description: Only bloodtusks that survive the Contest of Conquest are deemed worthy enough to become a mount of the Undying Army.

name: Court Sinrunner
description: Sinrunners were once the steeds of dark souls, carried in the wake of either their sins or their masters' sins to Revendreth. While some chose to run free, many continue to serve darkness in death as they did in life.

name: Battle-Bound Warhound
description: Warhounds find comfort in conflict, and the Contest of Conquest is the perfect place to find these beasts honing their abilities.

name: Battle-Hardened Aquilon
description: The aquilon can be a valuable ally in combat, with capabilities exceeding those of the phalynx.

name: Skyskin Hornstrider
description: The instability of primalist storms in potential timelines sometimes causes creatures to absorb the energies from the very storms. These creatures tend to be docile towards those who are able to face the fury of these chronomatic anomalies.

name: Ardenweald Wilderling
description: As anima flows to the distant wealds of Ardenweald once more, the wilderlings awaken and cautiously emerge from their canopy burrows.

name: Autumnal Wilderling
description: The wilderlings are benevolent, magical creatures of Ardenweald, and are drawn to souls that are strong and pure.

name: Winter Wilderling
description: Wilderlings serve as support units in battle, preserving their allies and rendering enemy magics useless.

name: Dauntless Duskrunner
description: The Path of Ascension is a challenging trial, and those that display all of the facets the Kyrian hold dear will be duly rewarded.

name: Sintouched Deathwalker
description: Forged from the sins of countless diabolical dungeon denizens. Only those with the skills to overcome them may hold its reins.

name: Pale Acidmaw
description: The acid present in the ichor of this creature sapped away the color from its shell, leaving it a striking shade of snowy white.

name: Marrowfang
description: Marrowfang, Bonefang's progeny, was respected by Amarth the Harvester for her insatiable appetite, even if it disrupted any experiments that the sadistic commander wanted to run.

name: Callow Flayedwing
description: Flayedwings are a necessary force in the cycle of life for the Shadowlands, acting as both prey and predator for the harsh environment of Maldraxxus.

name: Gruesome Flayedwing
description: The flayedwings of Maldraxxus are prized for their ability to soar effortlessly through the skies of the Shadowlands, allowing their rider a surprise opportunity to strike at their foes or support their allies.

name: Bonesewn Fleshroc
description: Only expert flesh-stitchers who have demonstrated mastery over the magics of Maldraxxus are powerful enough to construct a deathroc of this size.

name: Hulking Deathroc
description: Deathrocs are stitched monstrosities from the discarded viscera, bone, and organs that were not required to construct soldiers for the House of Constructs.

name: Predatory Plagueroc
description: Gieger's everlasting hunger is legendary amongst the House of Constructs, and no beast is too large or bony to fit inside its massive stomach.

name: Arboreal Gulper
description: The wood and foliage growing atop this toad is a natural part of its body, helping it siphon anima from the air to nourish itself.

name: Vespoid Flutterer
description: A flutterer is an overgrown vespoid worker created to carry a rider. Rather than looking to a queen for leadership, it looks to its rider to guide it in all things.

name: Deepstar Aurelid
description: As one of the last remaining polyps of an elusive creature, this aurelid is at home in the water as much as the air.

name: Quawks
description: Squawks's older (and somehow louder) brother appears to tolerate your presence. For now. You should probably track down some crackers while you still can.

name: Highwind Darkmane
description: The Forsworn and their mounts take on a darker aspect upon renouncing the path laid forth by the Archon. While tame enough to ride, the Highwind Darkmane has a fiercely independent streak.

name: Steamscale Incinerator
description: A mad gnomish inventor had a dream to ride a dragon, so he created this metal monstrosity. He powered it with a trapped demon, in order to create "an authentic dragon soul," and did not survive its activation.

name: Amber Ardenmoth
description: The brilliant orange hue of the amber ardenmoth signals to predators that it is dangerous to eat. Consuming one of these creatures will put a predator into a dreamless sleep for years.

name: Vibrant Flutterwing
description: These minty green moths are very friendly. They particularly enjoy having their head fuzz vigorously brushed. They also enjoy music and poetry.

name: Summer Wilderling
description: Stubborn and rebellious, this wilderling is loyal to very few. But those few are bonded for life.

name: Sinful Gladiator's Soul Eater
description: This mount shows a proclivity for snacking upon the souls cast out of Revendreth.

name: Sinrunner Blanchy
description: In life, Blanchy appeared to be an affectionate, well-behaved horse of excellent disposition and appetite. What evils must she have committed in secret to have earned her place among the condemned souls of Revendreth?

name: Unsuccessful Prototype Fleetpod
description: This creature was not designed by the First Ones, but instead by an unknown being attempting to create a very fast runner. Needless to say the attempt failed, because this mount is very slow.

name: Scarlet Helicid
description: This helicid's unusual reddish tint is clearly intentional in its design, but the only other difference between these and other helicids is the unusual lack of any ooze.

name: Deathrunner
description: These wild cervids are only truly at peace when running through--or between--the lands of Death.

name: Hailstorm Armoredon
description: Born in the middle of an ice storm, this armoredon prefers its meals partially frozen.

name: Sundered Zerethsteed
description: Whatever the purpose of these cervids once was, it is long forgotten. Though they are not Dominated, they have been corrupted by the unintended presence of Mawsworn in the sacred realm of Zereth Mortis.

name: Anointed Protostag
description: Only those most esteemed by the Enlightened are trusted with the reins of this rare cervid.

name: Carcinized Zerethsteed
description: The few of these mysterious creatures that remain appear to be impossibly old, almost timeless. Perhaps they were created by the First Ones, because no schematic to synthesize these has yet been discovered.

name: Tangled Dreamweaver
description: The last of the sleepers are finally awakening, bringing the dreams of the dragons back to the lands.

name: Bestowed Thunderspine Packleader
description: A gift from Merithra, who blessed these thunderspines to ensure that no matter where the Maruuk clans roam, they will always be under the green dragonflight's protection.

name: Heartlight Vombata
description: The Enlightened revere all creations of the First Ones, yet for many, the vombata of Zereth Mortis are the most beloved. These creatures are equal parts trusted beast of burden and beloved companion.

name: Bestowed Ottuk Vanguard
description: A gift from Brena, the Iskaara sent their most loyal (and adorable) companions to watch over Kalecgos and his reuinited family in Valdrakken

name: Brown War Ottuk
description: The tuskarr of Iskaara are loathe to war against anyone, but many tuskarr and their allies have nonetheless taken up the spear with a war ottuk beside them.

name: Otterworldly Ottuk Carrier
description: Some ottuks get into the spirit of carrying people around more than others.

name: Yellow War Ottuk
description: The yellow ottuks of the Azure Span are sleek, sneaky, and smart. Those trained for war are the finest tuskarr cavalry mounts, equally capable on the ground and in the waters.

name: Wondrous Wavewhisker
description: They say one of these magical fish is born when a fisherfolk dreams of the one that got away... and grows bigger with each retelling of the tale.

name: Lord of the Corpseflies
description: To be the Lord of the Corpseflies, you've got to lay a LOT of eggs.

name: Big Slick in the City
description: Big heart, big dreams, Big Slick.

name: Igneous Shalewing
description: You'd be forgiven for mistaking this creature for a flying volcanic rock. Because to some extent, it is.

name: Cobalt Shalewing
description: Brood of Karokta, raised in the underlight of Zaralek Cavern.

name: Calescent Shalewing
description: A shalewing born in the wake of Fyrakk's destructive breach. Cursed by his primal flame, its temperature slowly but constantly rises. It must immerse itself in water to cool off periodically, lest its own heat smother it.

name: Shadowflame Shalewing
description: Born from the magma, its carapace was originally molten blobs cooled to form dark-colored volcanic rock. Being imbued by shadowflame has only solidified its unbreakable nature.

name: Cataloged Shalewing
description: The first instance of a flaming shalewing ever discovered. Nine out of ten researchers agree: it's hot!

name: Boulder Hauler
description: These shalewings have been specially trained to carry large amounts of Barter Boulders for the niffen's bartering activities.

name: Sandy Shalewing
description: The patterns on the wings of that creature resemble sand flowing through an hourglass. One could gaze upon them for ages...

name: Morsel Sniffer
description: A breed of shalewings trained by the niffen to find the deepest of morsels.

name: Soaring Razorwing
description: The hide of the razorwing is light, allowing it to fly, but also quite tough, allowing them to survive passage into the Maw.

name: Forged Spiteflyer
description: There's nothing that can make you feel as alive quite like riding through the realm of Death while atop a being that hates you.

name: Buzz
description: Buzz buzz buzz, buzz buzz! Buzz buzz buzz buzz buzz. Buzz buzz buzz, buzz buzz buzz.

name: Jigglesworth Sr.
description: It's unclear how the saddle stays atop this formerly feline friend, but somehow the House of Plagues made it work. Science.

name: Bound Blizzard
description: A flurrious, fridgid friend, frost-flecked for frozen fun.

name: Curious Crystalsniffer
description: Most vombata enjoy tunneling through the ground, but these sniffers seem to have an unerring ability to locate crystals buried deep underground and will congregate around large deposits.

name: Darkened Vombata
description: The schematic to synthesize these darkened vombata seem to have appeared as a reaction to the Mawsworn invasion. The resulting vombata look different--darker, much like the invaders--but behave normally.

name: Adorned Vombata
description: Red-colored vombata are considered lucky by the Enlightened. It was a red vombata that was present when they first arrived in Zereth Mortis.

name: Tazavesh Gearglider
description: Brokers of all types use these gliders to travel around Tazavesh and similar nearby outposts, and carry their goods as well.

name: Genesis Crawler
description: The Enlightened who have observed tarachnids believe that this variety may be some of the first beings to ever walk the land of Zereth Mortis.

name: Tarachnid Creeper
description: These tarachnids make their home in the darkest corners of Zereth Mortis, coming out only to hunt (or, sometimes, carry a rider around).

name: Ineffable Skitterer
description: These tarachnids move with a purpose unknown to others, answering only to a higher calling. Whatever that may be, it appears to include routine assistance to mortals who need a ride. For now.

name: Wastewarped Deathwalker
description: Raw swirling energy bound together into a cohesive form, it will only bend to the will of those strong enough to bind it.

name: Goldplate Bufonid
description: This armored bufonid is always ready to leap into battle... as long as that battle is really, really close. In a pinch, it can leap multiple times and eventually reach the battle.

name: Liberated Slyvern
description: It's difficult to tell because it's far too angry, but this slyvern is actually glad it's not owned by gnolls anymore.

name: Sapphire Vorquin
description: Respected for their resilience in battle, even against dragonfire, these noble beasts are the prized mounts of the dracthyr cavalry.

name: Crimson Vorquin
description: Respected for their resilience in battle, even against dragonfire, these noble beasts are the prized mounts of the dracthyr cavalry.

name: Bronze Vorquin
description: Respected for their resilience in battle, even against dragonfire, these noble beasts are the prized mounts of the dracthyr cavalry.

name: Obsidian Vorquin
description: Respected for their resilience in battle, even against dragonfire, these noble beasts are the prized mounts of the dracthyr cavalry.

name: Val'sharah Hippogryph
description: These majestic hippogryphs are as close to a being of the Emerald Dream as any resident of Azeroth can be, and do not bond with a rider easily.

name: Bronzewing Vespoid
description: Bronzewing vespoids are the result of attempting to synthesize a vespoid queen with no other members of a new hive. While no mortal has yet discovered how to synthesize a hive, the bronzewings are reliable mounts just as they are.

name: Mawdapted Raptora
description: Since the Jailer's incursion, new raptora have been synthesized by Zereth Mortis that are capable of resisting the corrupting influence of the Maw.

name: Raptora Swooper
description: The raptora swooper is one of the most vicious iterations of its kind, perfectly suited for its ecological niche. Needing no further adjustments, the ones synthesized today are identical to those created in the past.

name: Bronze Helicid
description: Some helicids of Zereth Mortis have strangely metallic shells. The ones who do are generally a bronze color, indistinct from the metal any novice miner could work, save that this creature's construction far surpasses the skill of any novice.

name: Wen Lo, the River's Edge
description: The rivers of Pandaria, like Wen Lo, are unpredictable in nature. They may remain calm, bringing water to the crops, or flood, bringing ruin. Wen Lo reminds us that we must adapt to the challenges of life.

name: Soaring Spelltome
description: In the forgotten depths of the Dalaran library, this oversized tome was found flapping madly around the room. Open the pages of this arcane-infused book and hitch a ride to magical adventure.

name: Guardian Vorquin
description: Trained using ancient dracthyr methods, these armored vorquin are the pinnacle of tactical mounted ground warfare.

name: Swift Armored Vorquin
description: Trained using ancient dracthyr methods, these armored vorquin are the pinnacle of tactical mounted ground warfare.

name: Armored Vorquin Leystrider
description: Trained using ancient dracthyr methods, these armored vorquin are the pinnacle of tactical mounted ground warfare.

name: Majestic Armored Vorquin
description: Trained using ancient dracthyr methods, these armored vorquin are the pinnacle of tactical mounted ground warfare.

name: Bestowed Ohuna Spotter
description: A gift from the Ohn'ir, these ohuna are invaluable companions when scouting new horizons for the Dragonscale Expedition.

name: Duskwing Ohuna
description: If you look to the night sky and see the clouds whisk by don't panic. It may just be the illusive Duskwing Ohuna.

name: Zenet Hatchling
description: The hardest winds begin with a gust and a flap.

name: Wild Hunt Legsplitter
description: Integrated into the depleted forces of the Wild Hunt, the fawn riders quickly found that the gorm's powerful mandibles were perfect for cutting down close enemy ranks.

name: Voracious Gorger
description: The devourers are presumed to have their origins in the In-Between, a space between the lands of the Shadowlands that few have explored.

name: Vicious War Croaker
description: These creatures are so enthusiastic when charging into battle that they will croak out something that sounds very much like "FOR THE HORDE!"

name: Vicious War Croaker
description: These creatures are so enthusiastic when charging into battle that they will croak out something that sounds very much like "FOR THE ALLIANCE!"

name: Obsidian Gravewing
description: In the darkened skies of Revendreth, you cannot see these creatures coming until it is too late.

name: Sinfall Gravewing
description: With anima once again flowing in Sinfall, the Stonewright has been able to infuse these Stoneborn, making them valuable allies in the battles to come.

name: Pale Gravewing
description: Gravewings know no fear and are nearly indestructible -- perfect for an assault on the Maw.

name: Elysian Aquilon
description: With anima once again flowing to the Eternal Forge in Bastion, the plans for the aquilon centurion could be crafted once more.

name: Forsworn Aquilon
description: Originally produced by the Forsworn with stolen blueprints, these powerful centurion are now earning a reputation across the Shadowlands.

name: Ascendant's Aquilon
description: The aquilon can rapidly transport aspirants, stewards, and even ascended to a location. Hey, sometimes your wings get tired.

name: Maldraxxian Corpsefly
description: Corpseflies were once found throughout Maldraxxus, but many died out in the absence of anima. With anima flowing once again, they swarm.

name: Regal Corpsefly
description: Only the forces of Maldraxxus would call a corpsefly "regal."

name: Battlefield Swarmer
description: Corpseflies are strongly attracted to the smell of dead bodies and will swarm an active battlefield as soon as the first corpse falls.

name: Shimmering Aurelid
description: Though it was once created to defend the Sepulcher of the First Ones, with its primary function now completed, it now obediently follows your command.

name: Eternal Gladiator's Soul Eater
description: This soul eater's gaze unnerves you. Best not meet its eyes for too long.

name: Depthstalker
description: Poached from Zereth Mortis, a few of these jellyfish were lost when the poachers' ship sank. They found a new home in the Great Sea's depths.

name: Cryptic Aurelid
description: The Cyphers of the First Ones are beyond any mortal understanding. A curious effect of the language is that, for reasons unknown, some aurelids are drawn to mortals who aspire to unlock its mystery.

name: Serenade
description: These beautiful helicids emerge only at night. When they are pleased, their shells hum with a joyful melody.

name: Sanctum Gloomcharger
description: Only the Jailer's most powerful lieutenants are gifted these steeds to stalk the halls of the Tower.

name: Soulbound Gloomcharger
description: Ve'nari has many methods of sneaking around the Maw and disrupting the Jailer's plans, including binding his own minions.

name: Fallen Charger
description: The charger's screams echo across the Maw as it continues a never-ending ride across the land, forever looking for escape from the endless torments it has been forced to endure.

name: Hand of Nilganihmaht
description: Of all the giants who dared defy the Jailer, Nilganihmaht was the most arrogant. For this transgression, the giant's severed hand was hidden within a veil of torments, and the rings that could summon it back were scattered across the Maw.

name: Hand of Salaranga
description: Salaranga was the most cunning of her kin and hid amongst the Beastwarrens. She struck at the Banished One with a horde of bestial allies. It did not matter.

name: Amber Shardhide
description: The bright colors of Korthian wildlife indicate that they have either few predators, or are tough enough to fight back.

name: Crimson Shardhide
description: When Korthia was pulled into The Maw, only a few hardy species survived.

name: Darkmaul
description: An older shardhide, Darkmaul was relatively tame and friendly around Korthians. He was thought lost to the calamity.

name: Fierce Razorwing
description: Razorwings generally stay away from other Korthian species, but with time and patience, they can be tamed as well.

name: Garnet Razorwing
description: Korthia was once known for its bright red flowers. Several species were known for consuming or rolling in them, and taking on bright red coloration as a result.

name: Dusklight Razorwing
description: Razorwings can be seen throughout Korthia, making their nests on high hills and in trees.

name: Maelie, the Wanderer
description: A curious and inquisitive doe, she doesn't mean to worry her master, she just gets distracted easily. Especially with all of the new things to see on Korthia.

name: Unchained Gladiator's Soul Eater
description: This soul eater's gaze unnerves you. Best not meet its eyes for too long.

name: Tamed Mauler
description: When it comes to taming devourers, all it takes is a little anima and a lot of patience. And a lot of healing potions.

name: Noble Bruffalon
description: It is a great honor to be trusted with one of the Maruuk's loyal hunting companions. This Bruffalon is reliable and steadfast through any hardship.

name: Beryl Shardhide
description: These shardhides find it easy to hide in the underbrush, and it is rare to find one that is tamed.

name: Vicious Sabertooth
description: The elite Alliance forces that use these sabertooth mounts use a battle cry that translates from Darnassian as "here kitty kitty".

name: Sapphire Skyblazer
description: These brilliant blue birds burn brighter (and hotter) than their orange cousins, though the origin of both remains a mystery.

name: Amber Skitterfly
description: A gift from Granpap Whiskers to the Trading Post, amber skitterflies are remarkably docile.

name: Vicious Sabertooth
description: The elite Horde forces that use these sabertooth mounts use a battle cry that translates from Thalassian as "pspspspsps".

name: Arcberry Manasaber
description: Named for their resemblance to the bright berries made into arcwine, these proud cats have a keen awareness of the ley lines that travel beneath them.

name: Undying Darkhound
description: The Undying Darkhound's parts have graced many battlefields, yet this latest battle may have been the first time they all fought together.

name: Vengeance
description: Sylvanas lost a part of herself the day Arthas took her life, and the darkness within her grew to fill the void.

name: Soultwisted Deathwalker
description: A howling vortex of tormented souls, ridden only by those who wish to demonstrate their impressive tolerance for pain and suffering.

name: Savage Green Battle Turtle
description: Turtle too slow to chase down your foes? Just add cannons. Nothing can outrun a cannon.

name: Viridian Phase-Hunter
description: Hailing from a species already adept at natural camouflage, the otherworldly energies that transformed Draenor into Outland gave these creatures the ability to shift through space.

name: Slime Serpent
description: This mysterious creature lurks beneath the slime pools of Plaguefall. No one knows where it came from.

name: Vicious Warstalker
description: Trample the Horde to dust while riding a majestic protoform wolf! For the Alliance!

name: Vicious Warstalker
description: Trample the Alliance to dust while riding a majestic protoform wolf! For the Horde!

name: Grimhowl
description: Otherwise known as The Flame Woof, the Fetcher of the Igneous, Molten Core's Best Friend, Forgehound Maximus, the Best Fiery Boy, and "oh no is that an AXE on his face!?"

name: Vicious War Gorm
description: The gorm love eating the corpses of your enemies, so this seemed like a natural fit.

name: Sarge's Tale
description: Hold on a sec, Sarge isn't that large, someone must have spun a tall tale in the Hearthstone Tavern...

name: Rampaging Mauler
description: One would think controlling a rampaging mount would be difficult, but if you can learn to control the direction of the rampage it can be quite useful.

name: Vicious War Gorm
description: The gorm love eating the corpses of your enemies, so this seemed like a natural fit.

name: Cartel Master's Gearglider
description: Only the richest cartel leaders are able to afford the metals required to craft the Cartel Master's Gearglider.

name: Xy Trustee's Gearglider
description: Cartel Xy trades in many currencies; none are as valuable to its inner circle as loyalty.

name: Vandal's Gearglider
description: A rarer, customized glider most commonly used for clandestine gatherings. Its silence makes it near impossible to detect, making it a favored tool for those who work in the shadows.

name: Magmashell
description: Who's a good little new-forged snail made entirely of elemental lava? You are! Yes, you!

name: Hand of Bahmethra
description: The most wicked and cruel of her kin, Bahmethra endured the full measure of the Banished One's punishments before her fall.

name: Fierce Grimlynx
description: This grimlynx has seen many battles against the creatures in the Rift of Aln.

name: Rootstalker Grimlynx
description: With fur to match the deep roots and armor to match the vast foliage, these Grimlynx are equipped to be some of the ultimate hunters of Harandar.

name: Wandering Ancient
description: A staunch defender of the forests of Azeroth, the ancients are also known for their glorious canopies of leaves, which change color with the seasons.

name: Divine Kiss of Ohn'ahra
description: ...as though welcomed by the wind itself.

name: Iskaara Trader's Ottuk
description: There are no guarantees when you purchase an ottuk from Tattukiaka. At least they're cute.

name: Telix the Stormhorn
description: Once a simple beetle of the Forbidden Reach, Telix became infused with storm magic when Raszageth was unleashed. With its newly heightened senses, Telix roams the Dragon Isles alongside the Dragonscale Expedition--and you!

name: Frostfall Groveglider
description: These high-flying denizens of Azeroth's tallest trees have developed a thick, silky coat to adapt to even the coldest climates.

name: Zereth Overseer
description: Enigmatic and aloof, this construct of the First Ones flies on a graceful stream of perfect geometric understanding.

name: Colossal Umbrahide Mawrat
description: This disturbingly large Mawrat is an anomaly amongst its species. These Colossal Mawrats lurk only in the most distant and darkest reaches of Torghast.

name: Colossal Ebonclaw Mawrat
description: This vicious and cunning creature has grown to its astonishing size by not only feasting upon the weakened souls in the Jailer's Tower, but also vulnerable Mawsworn.

name: Winding Slitherdrake
description: These drakes feel at home in the depths of the earth and the far reaches of the sky.

name: Renewed Proto-Drake
description: As the isles awaken, these proto-drakes answer the call to soar the skies alongside mortals.

name: Gleaming Moonbeast
description: Armored in glistening silver, these devoted mounts are said to be a blessing from Elune herself.

name: Rocket Shredder 9001
description: The salesgoblin always tells people about the RS-9001's huge variety of attachments, but somehow neglects to mention that you never get to pick the attachments it'll have when you mount up.

name: Treetop Highmountain Eagle
description: The majestic eagle is a symbol of pride for the Highmountain tribes. This great beast's ability to carry tauren on their backs speaks to the sheer strength and size of the creatures of Highmountain.

name: Elusive Emerald Hawkstrider
description: Unseen for many years, this brightly hued hawkstrider emerged from hiding to help defend the Ghostlands from the Scourge. Its prowess shines as brilliantly as its feathers.

name: Highland Drake
description: The drakes of Azure Span follow a strict personal code of honor as they explore the isles anew.

name: Windborne Velocidrake
description: The long talons of the velocidrake give it an edge on the plains.

name: Loyal Magmammoth
description: Liberated from the Qalashi and harnessed by your ingenuity (and your harness), this magmammoth is ready to lava stomp wherever you want to go.

name: Stormtouched Bruffalon
description: Even from within her prison, Raszageth's primal scorn brought the mightiest beasts to heel.

name: Cliffside Wylderdrake
description: Often roosting along the steep cliff faces of Thaldraszus, they're swift to the skies in times of danger.

name: Gooey Snailemental
description: Formed from the leftover elemental energies of the Primal Storms, this magical mollusk is faster than it appears.

name: Crimson Gladiator's Drake
description: Forged in the fires of conquest, and armored to the teeth.

name: Tamed Skitterfly
description: It didn't take long for Granpap Whiskers to tame the skitterflies around the Dragonscale Basecamp and set them to helping move Expedition personnel and cargo.

name: Starspark Netherdrake
description: After flying you to the ends of Outland and beyond, the netherwing drake was eventually drawn to the blue skies of Azeroth as well.

name: Scrappy Worldsnail
description: For the Worldbreakers, a portable footstool. For you, a hardy mount.

name: Frostbrood Proto-Wyrm
description: The same magic of undeath that raised the Frostbrood into the Lich King's service has infused this primal wyrm. Its loyalty and ferocity now serve those who defend Azeroth.

name: Azure Skitterfly
description: Once Granpap Whiskers finally taught them how to take riders, the azure skitterflies proved to bond closely and loyally to their riders.

name: Verdant Skitterfly
description: Verdant skitterflies are the perfect mount for the scouts of the Dragonscale Expedition scouts, as they are fast and blend into the foliage of the Dragon Isles.

name: Bestowed Trawling Mammoth
description: A gift from Scout Tomul, so that the feasts of Iskaara will always have enough fish to feed those in need.

name: Dragon Isles Drake Model Test
description: NYI

name: Coral-Stalker Waveray
description: This species of ray seems to have evolved to stalk its prey amidst the brightly colored corals of the seas.

name: Nether-Gorged Greatwyrm
description: Few mana wyrms find sufficiently stable sources of arcane power to reach such a size. A greatwyrm's appetite for mana is unmatched.

name: Bestowed Sandskimmer
description: A gift from Granpap Whiskers, these capricious skitterflies help the Timewalkers clean up present (and future) sand-related temporal calamities.

name: Ancient Salamanther
description: Due to their preference for dark caves and murky waters, sightings of this unique breed of salamanther are so rare that many researchers thought they had gone extinct.

name: Mossy Mammoth
description: Preserved for thousands of years in stone, bronze magic has nearly aged this mammoth back into a fossil.

name: Lizi, Thunderspine Tramper
description: Nothing will stop Lizi from her mission to eat all the world's fruit.

name: Coralscale Salamanther
description: As Raszageth's storms abated, this aquatic breed of salamanther was among the many wonders to settle among the shores in their wake.

name: Plainswalker Bearer
description: The mammoths of the Ohn'ahran Plains are unaccustomed to carrying people, as their centaur neighbors do not typically ride mounts. They can learn to bear riders, however. Some even do so enthusiastically.

name: Colossal Plaguespew Mawrat
description: These rodents became Iska's infamous sentries, scouting and carrying her plague across the Maw. Their presence is only followed by ruin.

name: Colossal Wraithbound Mawrat
description: Infused by the energies of tormented souls, these creatures have adopted ghostlike abilities that make them quite elusive.

name: Armored Golden Pterrordax
description: Painted and adorned with gold, these Pterrordax patrol the skies over the tomb of Atal'Dazar.

name: Stormhide Salamanther
description: Sometimes when storm energies infuse a creature, it simply... becomes larger and more placid. Few salamanthers are willing to be ridden, but these children of the storm are a notable exception.

name: Otto
description: Relax. The future will unfold as it was meant to, one way or an otter.

name: Brown Scouting Ottuk
description: Wily and playful in their natural habitat, ottuks possess keen eyes that can scout far afield as ably as they watch for predators.

name: Ivory Trader's Ottuk
description: No adult knows why some ottuks are white, but any tuskarr child can tell you: it's because they eat too much snow.

name: Yellow Scouting Ottuk
description: The yellow scouting ottuk love to hide and they blend in well to their surroundings. When not 'playing' hide and seek with their riders, they are perfect companions when scouting enemy encampments.

name: Explorer's Stonehide Packbeast
description: A true explorer never stays in one place for long. Having a reliable packbeast with a strong back and sturdy legs is essential for travel.

name: Subterranean Magmammoth
description: Awakened from a long slumber, this beast has yet to see the light of day.

name: Cosmic Gladiator's Soul Eater
description: This soul eater's gaze unnerves you. Best not meet its eyes for too long.

name: Raging Magmammoth
description: Most lava flows. This lava charges.

name: Renewed Magmammoth
description: Reforged after destruction at the hands of the Druids of the Flame, this magmammoth is ready to stomp any foes in its path.

name: Patient Bufonid
description: Some bufonids will sit perfectly still for hours or days at a time if left to their own devices. Fortunately, they're also quite agreeable if a rider would like to move more often than that.

name: Prototype Leaper
description: An ancient schematic allows for the instantiation of this early, but functional, bufonid design.

name: Cerulean Marsh Hopper
description: Gottum's expansion into export markets was inevitable with news of the Trading Post reaching even into the depths of the Frogmarsh.

name: Seething Slug
description: Drawn forth from its abyss by the rage of the Zaqali.

name: Russet Bufonid
description: Russet is a rare color among bufonids that is normally only seen on specimens that are tainted by other cosmic forces, or synthesized from a perplexing schematic that invokes that color.

name: Jade, Bright Foreseer
description: The presence of this majestic Pandaren rabbit spirit brings joy and happiness and is heralded as a wonderful omen. Legend says that in its eyes, you can glimpse the greatest happiness of your life, even if it is yet to come.

name: Colossal Soulshredder Mawrat
description: The incredible prowess you have demonstrated within the Jailer's Tower has instilled sufficient respect into this fearsome creature, enabling its use as a mount.

name: Heartbond Lupine
description: From the moment of its creation, this packless lupine has held nothing more than deepest affection and loyalty towards you.

name: Shellack
description: Fed by victory and hardened by glory, this snail is nothing less than a tribute to mastery.

name: Amalgam of Rage
description: The fearsome beast ridden by Astaroth himself is now yours to command.

name: Fel-Scarred Mana Ray
description: Surviving on Argus is not an easy task. Its leathery hide scarred by fel corruption, this mana ray is prone to fits of aggression, moreso than usual.

name: Tuskarr Shoreglider
description: The Tuskarr learned long ago that some of the bones of the sea creatures they hunted were light and buoyant, making for effective glider materials.

name: Swift Spectral Drake
description: Even the dead need a lift every now and then.

name: Valiance
description: Most undead steeds have their riders chosen for them. Valiance, on the other hand, chooses its own rider.

name: Felstorm Dragon
description: The Burning Legion defeated dragons in a distant timeway. These foul fel wyrms chose corruption and servitude over destruction.

name: Polly Roger
description: The scourge of the slightly-northeastern seas, giant parrots like Polly can live for up to 2000 years. What a wonderful curse on your next of kin.

name: Inferno Armoredon
description: Its tail smells like campfire but its breath smells like brimstone.

name: Tarecgosa's Visage
description: "Farewell, Tarecgosa. A dear sister, hero, student, and teacher. Though blood does not bind us, you will always be family. May your soul find its place soaring among the stars."

name: Obsidian Gladiator's Slitherdrake
description: This mischievous drake enjoys flying through geysers and volcanic plumes.

name: Vicious War Snail
description: The Alliance uses these ravenous snail mounts to traverse over any obstacle in battlefields.

name: Grotto Netherwing Drake
description: After traveling to Outland and back, it's only natural these drakes seek out new skies.

name: Ancestral Clefthoof
description: This clefthoof has seen ages pass, witnessing everything and patiently teaching many to ride.

name: Felcrystal Scorpion
description: The results of a fel infusion experiment with one of Skorpyron's offspring.

name: Bloodtooth Mana Ray
description: The shattering of Argus drove this breed of mana ray to bloodthirst. It now subsists on the corrupted blood of any creatures it can sink its many teeth into.

name: Vicious War Snail
description: The Horde uses these ravenous snail mounts to traverse over any obstacle in battlefields.

name: Highland Drake
description: The drakes of Azure Span follow a strict personal code of honor as they explore the isles anew.

name: Scarlet Pterrordax
description: This pterrordax was so eager to take to the skies that its handler couldn't finish training it.

name: White War Wolf
description: Can a wolf's hunger ever be satiated? These lupine brutes seek and devour the enemies of the Horde.

name: Sulfur Hound
description: The Black Empire subjugated these humbled and hateful hounds. Utterly obedient, they still serve the strong.

name: Renewed Proto-Drake
description: As the isles awaken, these proto-drakes answer the call to soar the skies alongside mortals.

name: Ravenous Black Gryphon
description: War corrupted these once-proud creatures. The susurration of their wings now whispers omens of imminent demise.

name: Windborne Velocidrake
description: The long talons of the velocidrake give it an edge on the plains.

name: Cliffside Wylderdrake
description: Often roosting along the steep cliff faces of Thaldraszus, they're swift to the skies in times of danger.

name: Winding Slitherdrake
description: These drakes feel at home in the depths of the earth and the far reaches of the sky.

name: Harbor Gryphon
description: This gryphon was the runt of its pride, left behind when its brethren were sent for training. Today, however, it is ready to prove its worth.

name: Royal Swarmer
description: These drones will swarm anyone who dares approach their queen uninvited.

name: Gold-Toed Albatross
description: Soaring from a distant timeway, this never-before-seen avian variant was born in a world of order.

name: Perfected Juggernaut
description: Perfection, like beauty, is in the eye of the beholder. But eyesight can be surgically enhanced.

name: Scourgebound Vanquisher
description: Once bound in service to the Scourge, these undead dragons exist only to spread death, disease, and destruction.

name: Verdant Armoredon
description: The gnarled horns of this armoredon are far stronger than bone.

name: Void-Razed Elekk
description: Their temperament is as wild as the twisted forces that warped their creation and they only serve who they choose.

name: Lively Darkmoon Charger
description: Draped in tattered circus banners, this eerie mount is a perfect blend of shadow and spectacle.

name: Archmage's Felscorned Disc
description: Some mages say fel magics are "volatile" or "corruptive" or "forbidden". Some mages are wrong.

name: Delugen
description: Imagine being so engrossed by a dream that it feels real; some might even call it a delusion.

name: Talont
description: Some daydreams are filled with wondrous possibilities, but shatter when reminded of reality's limitations. Some dreams are not so burdened, even when they carry a rider.

name: Stargrazer
description: One can't help but think what it might feel like to pluck the stars from their fixture in the sky. Perhaps, Stargrazer will show us how someday.

name: Chaos-Forged Wind Rider
description: Born and raised during a time of great chaos, this mount has survived hardships known by few.

name: Meeksi Rufflefur
description: Rufflefurs are playful, clumsy and obsessed with cleanliness. This is a dangerous combination of traits.

name: Meeksi Softpaw
description: Softpaws provide a black tea that is ideal for an early morning jolt or staying awake late into the night.

name: Legion-Forged Elekk
description: The Burning Legion envied the draenei's bond with the elekks of Draenor. Many demonic beast-masters warped the breed to serve their needs, but none could control them.

name: Kalu'ak Crest-Horn
description: The tuskarr do not call these Crest-Horn "Kalu'ak" to claim them, but as a warning they will not tolerate any more lost to poachers seeking their unique antlers.

name: Violet Darkmoon Charger
description: This steed carries the mystery and magic of the Darkmoon Faire on its back.

name: Bloodhunter Fel Bat
description: A beast from an unknown world within the Twisting Nether, tamed through brutal and relentless will.

name: Forest Windsteed
description: The fur of this Windsteed is not only colored to match its woodland home, but also usually contains bits of bark and leaves.

name: Dashing Windsteed
description: Windsteeds are incredibly gentle and easy to train... if you can catch one.

name: Feathered Windsurfer
description: "Check the current windspeed and elevation before mounting. Avoid trees and other tall objects. Damaged kites are ineligible for return or refund." - Windsurfer's Instruction Manual

name: Remembered Golden Gryphon
description: They love lions in Stormwind, and the gryphon is half lion. This is why these creatures are an air travel staple throughout the Alliance.

name: Meeksi Rollingpaw
description: Rollingpaws are very proud of their tea bearing duties. They are always willing to provide a warm cup of tea to their travelling companions.

name: Gilnean Prowler
description: A scrupulous breed of prowlers whose ferocity can only be matched by that of the worgen. These decisive creatures embody discipline and a controlled viciousness that even the worgen have come to admire.

name: The Big G
description: Doesn't actually seem like anything too special under the hood, but just wait until those goblins next door see you pull up for the next pool party.

name: Emerald Snail
description: This snail protects its treasures with the ferocity of a dragon, but perhaps not the speed.

name: Guardian Quilen
description: When Quan the Relentless fell in battle, his loyal hounds waited for his return. Millenia later, they remain patient.

name: Remembered Wind Rider
description: Hundreds of battles across a dozen campaigns have been fought and won on the backs of these honored creatures.

name: Meeksi Teatuft
description: Teatufts are known for their aromatic teas. However, their playful nature can result in an excess of spilled tea.

name: Shadow Dusk Dreamsaber
description: A dreamsaber strikes quickly from the shadows, capturing small animals and slipping back into darkness without a sound. The lucky few who have seen a feeding dreamsaber can see them revel in lazily toying with their prey before beginning their meal.

name: Astral Gladiator's Fel Bat
description: Awakened from caves in the battlefield, and armored to the fray.

name: Shimmermist Free Runner
description: Unburdened from the saddle and bit, these valerunners are truly free.

name: Marble Quilen
description: Created by a mogu sorcerer who breathed life into their favorite pet rock.

name: Gilded Riding Crane
description: It is said that an emperor once paid a large sum for a golden crane. Upon discovering that the crane was only colored like that of the precious metal, he hid with shame in his bedchambers for a week.

name: Pale Riding Crane
description: I looked, and behold, a pale... crane?

name: Rose Riding Crane
description: This brightly-colored bird is often confused with a flamingo or marabou.

name: Silver Riding Crane
description: This tall bird is one of the most graceful and elegant cranes, slightly moreso than the Bronze Crane but less than the Golden Crane.

name: Luxurious Riding Crane
description: A majestic bird, but a rather picky eater.

name: Meeksi Brewthief
description: Despite their mischievous and playful nature, these creatures make for loyal and dependable travelling companions.

name: Crimson Shreddertank
description: Experimental shredder designed for killing rather than harvesting.

name: Pearlescent Butterfly
description: Take to the skies on a glittering, shimmering, pearlescent butterfly.

name: Ruby Butterfly
description: The elusive Ruby Butterfly is highly sought after by Twilight Witches.

name: Winter Night Dreamsaber
description: These nocturnal dreamsabers carry the chill of winter with them and will often hibernate for days on end if not otherwise awoken.

name: Infinite Armoredon
description: Even among the armoredon, the possibilities for the future are infinite.

name: Void-Forged Stallion
description: Forged just as their shoes once were, these beasts are less horses now and void energy in their likeness.

name: Void-Scarred Pack Mother
description: Fighting her corrupted handlers this pack mother freed herself and her pups. However, freedom from their handlers did not free them from the void in this lost place. She taught her pups to be even more dangerous beasts, and they now stalk throughout Orgrimmar fetching her food as all compete to survive.

name: Ashplague Fel Bat
description: Only the mightiest of heroes ride into battle atop this monstrosity borne of fel and undeath.

name: Tropical Riding Crane
description: This Jade Forest native uses its coloration as camouflage.

name: Snowy Riding Goat
description: The Kun-Lai Summit mountain goats are known for climbing even the highest peaks in search of kafa beans and salt licks.

name: Little Red Riding Goat
description: What this goat lacks in size, it makes up for in stubbornness.

name: Bloody Skyscreamer
description: This dinosaur still harbors the rage of its ancestors from the Zandalari Invasion.

name: Crowd Pummeler 2-30
description: Guaranteed to knock the teeth out from your enemies with a single punch!

name: Machine Defense Unit 1-11
description: One of many such mechsuits designed to guard the Machine. After years of faithful service it is no longer suited for combat. It does make for an impressive conveyance, though.

name: Spring Butterfly
description: Take to the skies on a glittering, shimmering, spring butterfly.

name: Midnight Butterfly
description: PH

name: Enterprising Shreddertank
description: Optimized shredder designed for maximum shredding efficiency.

name: Floestrider Crest-Horn
description: The seldom seen native of the Azure Span is considered blessed by the tuskaar of the region.

name: Evening Sun Dreamsaber
description: These dreamsabers are ferocious in battle, yet lethargic and often found lazily outstretched upon open fields. After soaking in the warmth of daylight, their flame flares that much brighter.

name: Fur-endship Fox
description: These magical creatures thrive on love and friendship.

name: Soaring Sky Fox
description: Take to the sky on the most loyal and loving fox on Azeroth.

name: Twilight Sky Prowler
description: Dubbed the midnight trickster by some, many a couple point to him as the reason their paths crossed in the night.

name: Ginormous Grrloc
description: It's the second biggest murloc you've ever seen!

name: Clayscale Hornstrider
description: Named for their dusty-red appearance, clayscale hornstriders can be found tearing across the muddy hills of the Ohn'ahran Plains.

name: Night Pterrorwing
description: Scholars interpret this creature's awful screeching as "fear me, I am the night!"

name: Spotted Black Riding Goat
description: One time a farmhand walked by without petting this goat. That's all it took for the goat to make it personal.

name: Cragstepper Crest-Horn
description: Tuskaar aren't often found on Highmountain, but the crest-horns often come down to the shores to seek rare delicacies in the coastal waters.

name: Morning Flourish Dreamsaber
description: Known for the lavish energy that emanates from them, these sabers are notorious tricksters. Their roar scatters the early birds that wake them.

name: Void-Scarred Windrider
description: When the void energies were released, it drove most windriders feral. They killed each other and their trainers. The gates to their roost were locked by the survivors. Some surviving windriders can still be seen gliding above, but none dare return to their new deadly domain to see what remains.

name: Ny'alothan Shadow Worm
description: A remnant of the Black Empire's past, these precursors of the abyss worms are once again resurging with the rise of Ny'alotha.

name: Grotto Netherwing Drake
description: After traveling to Outland and back, it's only natural these drakes seek out new skies.

name: Flourishing Whimsydrake
description: These playful protectors of the Emerald Dream may be larger than their faerie dragon relatives, but they are no less mischievous.

name: Archmage's Great Raven
description: Perhaps a little older and a bit more gray instead of customary darkened midnight, this raven still represents one of the most powerful arcane masters of Azeroth, the Guardian Khadgar.

name: Tyrannotort
description: Vinnie Sweets kept this rare, albino turtle a secret, saving until it was time to help promote Dastardly Duos.

name: Corruption of the Aspects
description: With N'Zoth's triumph, what started with Galakrond comes to fruition. With the Aspects gone, the corruption of their species is complete.

name: Void-Crystal Panther
description: Although many precious gems have been corrupted by void energies in horrific visions, Master Jewelcrafters can still utilize them to craft mighty panther steeds.

name: Jade Pterrordax
description: This favored mount of the Zandalari is a great singer, or so it thinks.

name: Cobalt Juggernaut
description: What started as a rideable mining and logging machine devolved quickly into chaos when engineers asked themselves if they could make it any cooler.

name: Riverwalker Mushan
description: The Mushan is a voracious beast. Though it can be tamed, it will demand a second, third, and even fourth breakfast every day.

name: Herald of Sa'bak
description: The Tol'vir tell of the legendary Sa'bak, a great crocolisk believed to have emerged from the sacred valley of Un'Goro crater. Although few have ever seen Sa'bak in the flesh, many believe he was blessed by the Titan Keepers to safeguard the vast waterways of Uldum.

name: Void-Scarred Lynx
description: Taming lynxes is a practice that the Arathi have honed for years. While the Nightfall cultists remain devoted to their cause, they are still ones for passing that tradition along.

name: Umbral K'arroc
description: A savage K'arroc particularly well-suited to prowling the perpetual twilight of K'aresh.

name: K'arroc Swiftwing
description: A gift for services rendered, this K'arroc has seen its share of hazardous flights.

name: Lavender K'arroc
description: This particularly belligerent K'arroc's previous master died while investigating Untethered Space. Perhaps it will accept you instead... or maybe not.

name: The Bone Freezer
description: Bitter rival to The Bone Melter, this creeper was ousted from their lair near Manaforge Omega after a crushing defeat. It now seeks to learn from the one that dethroned its greatest foe.

name: Anu'relos, Flame's Guidance
description: Though her feathers flicker with flame's destructive potential, they do not scorch, but simply radiate a comfortable heat.

name: Jeweled Copper Scarab
description: The rulers of desert kingdoms would attune crystals to their personal mounts for quick summoning.

name: Amani Sunfeather
description: Born of Abundance and the forest's renewal, these eagles gleam in the setting sun, a living sign of the Amani's rekindled strength.

name: Amani Windcaller
description: Swift as the storm and sharp as lightning, these sacred eagles bear the mark of Akil'zon, carrying their riders on the winds of her blessing.

name: Vivacious Chloroceros
description: Catching a chloroceros mane leaf mid-air is considered a sign of good luck among the Haranir. The more vivacious the leaf, the greater the luck.

name: Vivid Chloroceros
description: The healthier the chloroceros, the more vivid the foliage.

name: Verdant Gladiator's Slitherdrake
description: This drake visited many heroes in their dreams before joining your cause.

name: Heartseeker Mana Ray
description: Someone once told this mana ray it was adorable. It has been pursuing that positive feeling ever since.

name: Fel Iron Juggernaut
description: This war machine was stolen from a timeline in which Garrosh Hellscream joined the Burning Legion. Suffice it to say, there is not much left of that timeline.

name: Purple Shado-Pan Riding Tiger
description: "When waking a tiger, use a long stick. Better yet, tell your friend to wake the tiger instead." -Pandaren Proverb

name: Palehide Mushan Beast
description: The Palehide Mushan is the tastiest of Mushan beasts, but it hates being reminded of that fact.

name: Runebound Firelord
description: By fire be purged the obstacles in your path!

name: Crimson Glimmerfur
description: Gilnean legend states this red-and-white fox only crosses the Winter Veil during one season.

name: Ruby Void Creeper
description: "Rescued" by the K'aresh Trust for study, this void creeper has turned out to be an incredibly sturdy mount.

name: Acidic Void Creeper
description: This creeper has feasted on noxious fumes resulting in its body being infused with powerful acids.

name: Blue Barry
description: A particularly rare variant of slateback, "Blue Barry" was previously used as a beast of burden.

name: Curious Slateback
description: This curious slateback somehow managed to get itself stuck in a box. How it fit inside is still up in the air.

name: Grandmaster's Prophetic Board
description: The chess event was not to be contained. So Medivh fashioned this conveyance to take it on trips.

name: Incognitro, the Indecipherable Felcycle
description: This demonic machine was designed by Chief Engineer Ulzik as an experiment in cross-dimensional traversal. Despite many deaths during testing, Ulzik remained confident that this vehicle would reveal secrets of the universe previously unknowable.

name: Golden Regal Scarab
description: Plated with gold and socketed with emeralds, this scarab is fit to fly royalty in style.

name: Jeweled Sapphire Scarab
description: Begifted to a young Tol'vir Princess, this ornate jeweled statue was brought to life by the Gemseers of Ramkahen.

name: Jeweled Jade Scarab
description: The razor-thin wings of this scarab are well-protected by a gleaming, yet indestructible jade-carved carapace.

name: Chrono Corsair
description: Always on the lookout for the next golden opportunity, this Infinite Dragonflight pirate has soared through the timeways to find a crew for the perfect heist.

name: Radiant Imperial Lynx
description: The Radiant Imperial Lynx embodies the ongoing strength and courage it takes to chase back the darkness. They hoist us up so that we may hold our torches high for all who seek refuge from the encroaching darkness.

name: Wretched Fel Bat
description: Its bracers seem to be a bit snug.

name: Voidwing Dragonhawk
description: Cutting through the skies, it flies with silent wings and eyes that glow with the echo of forgotten stars.

name: Lightwing Dragonhawk
description: Radiating warmth and grace, it soars on wings of light.

name: Unbound Star-Eater
description: In the void-scarred wake of the All-Devouring and his kith, twisted remnants of distant worlds and forgotten aeons find their way across the Great Dark.

name: Brown-Furred Spiky Bakar
description: Such a good boy. Or girl. It's hard to tell sometimes. Always good though.

name: Taivan
description: You don't have to be what the world expects you to be. Be who Taivan knows you *can* be.

name: August Phoenix
description: The magnificent August Phoenixes are blessed by Chi-Ji to fill the hearts of riders with wonder and hope for the future.

name: Grandmaster's Royal Board
description: Always moving forward to position yourself above the pawns below.

name: Felscorned Vilebrood Vanquisher
description: This flying amalgamation of the forces of death and fel pierces the skies with a bone-chilling screech.

name: Hateforged Blazecycle
description: This sweet deathcycle lets you go on your very own burning rubber crusade!
